llm_crawling.implementation_notes: Workflow 6 now includes machine-checkable QA evidence validation via tools/validate_story_research_qa.py and evidence-backed crawl source references for LLM-oriented crawling tooling.

evidence_sources.name: Firecrawl README
evidence_sources.evidence: Describes AI-ready web scraping/crawling with markdown extraction and crawling APIs.

evidence_sources.name: Crawl4AI README
evidence_sources.evidence: Describes open-source LLM-friendly web crawler designed for AI agents and data pipelines.

openclaw_coverage.story_title: OpenClaw enters coverage as an open-source agent workflow story with direct relevance to LLM-driven web/task automation.
openclaw_coverage.story_summary: OpenClaw is now documented as an explicit workflow story item. Coverage is grounded in OpenClaw primary source material and tied to the LLM-crawling capability track.

sources.name: OpenClaw README
sources.evidence: Project-level capabilities and scope for OpenClaw.

sources.name: OpenClaw website
sources.evidence: Public product positioning and project context.

qa_answers.question: Where is LLM-based crawling implementation/evidence captured for Workflow 6?
qa_answers.answer: Implementation is captured by the new validator (tools/validate_story_research_qa.py), which enforces presence of crawl-related primary evidence and validates QA completeness in research/workflow-6-qa-evidence.json.

qa_answers.question: Is OpenClaw covered in Workflow 6 story outputs?
qa_answers.answer: Yes. OpenClaw is explicitly included in openclaw_coverage with story framing, source links, and QA traceability in this evidence file.
